rulings.Case Title: EVENECEL VILLANUEVA et al VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK's DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges violation of CCC 2923.5. Defendant's minimal compliance with CCC 2923.5 (contact and some analysis of the borrower's financial situation) is not alleged in the complaint and does not appear in the judicially noticeable documents. At most Ex. F to Defendant's RJN shows that parties contemplated such contact and analysis to occur. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges standing under UCL and Plaintiff's default prior to the alleged violations in not alleged in complaint. See Jenkins v. JP Morgan Chase Bank (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 497. Defendant's arguments are more appropriate for a Motion for Summary Judgment. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 2. PETITIONERs SAMETC, LLC and JOHN SOLLNER's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award DENIED without prejudice. Petitioners failed to (1) provide evidence with their moving papers that condition of reconsideration order was satisfied; (2) cite any authority of fact showing that order re: multiple issues is a final award as there is no evidence as to what issues have been submitted to the arbitration, what issues have been finally resolved and for what issues arbitrator reserved jurisdiction for further review; (3) provide authority or point to a contractual provision authorizing Steven Block to appoint a successor arbitrator. The Court did not consider Respondent's submission of July 28, 2014 which was untimely and unfiled. The Court did not consider Petitioner's reply brief filed on July 29, 2014. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DIANA D. SAM VS. CATALINA BOYKIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTs DIANA SAM and MYRON MOSKOVITZ's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend to allege the covenant of the contract that was breached and how it was breached and to allege fraud damages. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLEN LEIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT GLEN LEIS' Motion To Quash DENIED. Motion is untimely per CCP 1987.1. Sanction's granted against defendant and his counsel in the amount of $1,595. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: MARINA GREEN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. GREG HERMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT GREG HERMAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: PLACE OF FRANK'S LLC, et al VS. ASHOK PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses And Production Of Documents In Regards To Defendants' First Requests To Plaintiffs And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And To Compel Attendance At Depositions Of Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANTs ASHOK PATEL and RICKESH PATEL's Motion To Compel Responses And Production Of Documents In Regards To Defendants' First Requests To Plaintiffs And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And To Compel Attendance At Depositions Of Plaintiffs GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide responses to written discovery without objections by personal delivery no later than 5:00 p.m. on July 31, 2014. If defendant's counsel is not available to accept service other means have to be arranged such as drop box. Frank Myers and Plaintiff's PMK shall appear for deposition on August 1, 2014. Frank Myers' deposition may be taken remotely. Any costs associated with remote deposition in excess of the cost of local deposition shall be born by the Plaintiff. If Plaintiff fails to comply with any portion of this order Defendant may re-new its motion to continue trial at the trial call on August 4, 2014. No sanctions. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: PLACE OF FRANK'S LLC, et al VS. ASHOK PATEL et al

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANTs ASHOK PATEL and RICKESH PATEL's Motion To Continue Trial And/Or Strike Plaintiffs' At Issue Memorandum DENIED without prejudice. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: BETTER PROPERTY MANAGEMENT VS. SHANE NEWELL et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT SHANE NEWELL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile the difference between the amount demanded on the operative notice and amount alleged to have been owed at the time of the notice in paragraph 17c. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: JCS PROPERTIES LLC VS. EDWARD NOVACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANT EDWARD NOVACK's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 1167.4. Also, moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD DUESTERBERG VS. ORFA HERRERA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 13. PLAINTIFF EDWARD DUESTERBERG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden and show that the Property is exempt from SFRO. Also, even if the burden is shifted there is a triable issue of fact re: whether defendant signed the lease attached to the Plaintiff's motion. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, line 11. DEFENDANT TANIA BHATIA's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons DENIED, 5 days to respond. Defendant was sub-served by a registered process server via sub-service on the doorman in control of the access to the premises and mailing. = (501/AJR) - LATE TENTATIVE
